$comment: Golden retrieval-evaluation set (#425). Corpus entries are seeded with REAL embeddings from the pinned ONNX model (ADR-017); queries score keyword and semantic retrieval via recall@5/@10 and MRR. Expected titles must match corpus titles exactly. Grow this set when a real agent query misses — add the query with its expected entry.

corpus.title: CS8618 non-nullable property warning on EF Core entities
corpus.body: EF Core entity classes with required string properties raise CS8618 nullable warnings. Use the required modifier or initialize with null! when the ORM guarantees materialization.

corpus.title: EF Core migration conflict after parallel feature branches
corpus.body: Two branches each adding a migration produce a model snapshot conflict. Re-scaffold the later migration after rebase: remove it, then run dotnet ef migrations add again.

corpus.title: IHttpClientFactory prevents socket exhaustion
corpus.body: Instantiating HttpClient per request leaks sockets in TIME_WAIT. Register typed clients through IHttpClientFactory so handlers are pooled and DNS changes are honored.

corpus.title: ConfigureAwait(false) guidance for ASP.NET Core
corpus.body: ASP.NET Core has no SynchronizationContext, so ConfigureAwait(false) is not required for correctness in application code; it still matters in general-purpose libraries.

corpus.title: System.Text.Json ignores private setters by default
corpus.body: Deserialization silently leaves properties with private setters at their default values. Mark them with JsonInclude or use init accessors.

corpus.title: BackgroundService swallows startup exceptions
corpus.body: An exception thrown in ExecuteAsync before the first await can be silently held until host shutdown. Await Task.Yield first or override StartAsync carefully so failures surface at startup.

corpus.title: xUnit parallelism causes Testcontainers port clashes
corpus.body: Parallel test collections each starting a PostgreSQL container can exhaust Docker resources and clash on host ports. Share one container per collection via ICollectionFixture.

corpus.title: dotnet format analyzers gate fails on IDE0005
corpus.body: Unused using directives fail the CI format gate with IDE0005. Run dotnet format analyzers locally before pushing.

corpus.title: ImagePullBackOff from private registry without imagePullSecrets
corpus.body: Pods stuck in ImagePullBackOff when pulling from a private registry: create a docker-registry secret and reference it via imagePullSecrets in the pod spec or service account.

corpus.title: CrashLoopBackOff diagnosis order
corpus.body: Check kubectl logs --previous first, then describe the pod for OOMKilled events, then liveness probe timing. Most crash loops are misconfigured probes or missing configuration.

corpus.title: PodDisruptionBudget blocks node drain
corpus.body: A PDB with minAvailable equal to the replica count makes kubectl drain hang forever. Lower minAvailable or scale up before node maintenance.

corpus.title: Readiness vs liveness probe semantics
corpus.body: Readiness gates traffic, liveness restarts the container. A failing readiness probe never restarts the pod; a failing liveness probe does.

corpus.title: Helm upgrade recreates renamed template resources
corpus.body: Renaming a template file changes resource identity, so helm upgrade deletes and recreates the object. Use the helm.sh/resource-policy keep annotation for stateful objects like PVCs.

corpus.title: NetworkPolicy default-deny breaks DNS resolution
corpus.body: A default-deny egress policy blocks kube-dns. Always pair it with an egress rule allowing UDP and TCP port 53 to the cluster DNS service.

corpus.title: pgvector HNSW index build needs maintenance_work_mem
corpus.body: CREATE INDEX USING hnsw can exceed maintenance_work_mem and fall back to a much slower build path. Raise maintenance_work_mem for the session before large index builds.

corpus.title: websearch_to_tsquery accepts raw user input safely
corpus.body: Unlike to_tsquery, websearch_to_tsquery never raises a syntax error on malformed input, making it safe to pass raw user query strings into full-text search.

corpus.title: Session state breaks under PgBouncer transaction pooling
corpus.body: Prepared statements and session-level SET break under transaction-mode pooling; use SET LOCAL inside a transaction and disable automatic prepared statements in the driver.

corpus.title: VACUUM cannot reclaim space while a long transaction holds xmin
corpus.body: A long-running transaction pins the xmin horizon and bloats tables because VACUUM cannot remove dead tuples. Monitor pg_stat_activity for idle in transaction sessions.

corpus.title: GIN index on a stored tsvector column
corpus.body: Store a generated tsvector column and index it with GIN. Expression indexes on to_tsvector over the raw column prevent the planner from using column statistics.

corpus.title: LOWER() comparison defeats a plain btree index
corpus.body: WHERE LOWER(col) = value cannot use a plain btree index on col and degrades to a sequential scan. Create a functional index on LOWER(col) or use citext.

corpus.title: set -euo pipefail caveats in bash strict mode
corpus.body: set -e does not fire inside command substitution used in conditions, and pipefail changes the exit status of pipelines. Know the exceptions before relying on strict mode.

corpus.title: shellcheck SC2086 word splitting on unquoted variables
corpus.body: Unquoted variable expansion splits on IFS and glob-expands. Quote every expansion unless splitting is intended.

corpus.title: trap EXIT for temp file cleanup
corpus.body: Create temp files with mktemp and remove them in a trap cleanup EXIT handler so failures and early returns still clean up.

corpus.title: BSD vs GNU sed -i incompatibility on macOS
corpus.body: macOS BSD sed requires an argument to -i (sed -i ''), GNU sed does not. Use a portable pattern or perl -pi -e for cross-platform scripts.

corpus.title: getopts stops at the first non-option argument
corpus.body: POSIX getopts does not permute arguments like GNU getopt; positional arguments placed before options terminate option parsing.

corpus.title: GitHub Actions cache key on lockfile hash
corpus.body: Key caches on hashFiles of the lockfile with restore-keys fallback prefixes, so a dependency bump misses cleanly while unrelated edits keep hitting.

corpus.title: Concurrency group cancels superseded workflow runs
corpus.body: Set concurrency with a group per ref and cancel-in-progress true to stop stale runs when new commits are pushed.

corpus.title: GITHUB_TOKEN events do not trigger downstream workflows
corpus.body: Events created with the default GITHUB_TOKEN do not trigger other workflows, preventing recursion. Use a fine-grained PAT or a GitHub App token for cascading automation.

corpus.title: Matrix jobs share nothing; pass outputs via artifacts
corpus.body: Each matrix job runs on a fresh runner. Upload artifacts or declare job outputs to move data between jobs; environment variables do not cross job boundaries.

corpus.title: Required status check names must match the job name exactly
corpus.body: Branch protection matches the reported check run name, not the workflow file name. Renaming a job silently detaches the required check.

corpus.title: Multi-stage build keeps the runtime image small
corpus.body: Build in an SDK stage and COPY --from the published output into a runtime-only base image to cut image size and attack surface.

corpus.title: BuildKit cache mounts for package restore
corpus.body: RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.nuget/packages persists package restore between builds without baking caches into image layers.

corpus.title: .dockerignore missing causes cache busts
corpus.body: COPY . . invalidates on every file change including .git. A proper .dockerignore keeps the build context small and the layer cache stable.

corpus.title: Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK vs orchestrator probes
corpus.body: Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK is ignored by Kubernetes, which uses its own probes. Chiseled and distroless images often lack a shell for CMD healthchecks anyway.

corpus.title: Rootless containers cannot bind privileged ports
corpus.body: Rootless Docker and Podman cannot bind ports below 1024 by default. Use higher host ports or set net.ipv4.ip_unprivileged_port_start.

corpus.title: OOMKilled container exit code 137
corpus.body: Exit code 137 means SIGKILL, commonly the kernel OOM killer or cgroup memory limits. Check docker inspect for OOMKilled and review memory limits.

expectedTitles: CS8618 non-nullable property warning on EF Core entities
ImagePullBackOff from private registry without imagePullSecrets
shellcheck SC2086 word splitting on unquoted variables
OOMKilled container exit code 137
websearch_to_tsquery accepts raw user input safely
Session state breaks under PgBouncer transaction pooling
BSD vs GNU sed -i incompatibility on macOS
dotnet format analyzers gate fails on IDE0005
pgvector HNSW index build needs maintenance_work_mem
GITHUB_TOKEN events do not trigger downstream workflows

queries.query: full text search index type postgres
expectedTitles: GIN index on a stored tsvector column
